verdict_reasoning: HSP70 is primarily cytoplasmic/nuclear at baseline but translocates to the outer PM under cell stress, heat shock, immunogenic death, and in tumor contexts. Surface HSP70 functions as a DAMP and is recognized by pattern-recognition receptors (LOX-1, SREC, TLR). The surfacing occurs via the protein's own stress-response translocation mechanism and exosomal release, not passive recruitment to other surface receptors. This is a well-characterized stress-induced and disease-associated surface form.
